title: Shinkansen
description: The Shinkansen, globally known as Japan's 'bullet train', is a network of high-speed railway lines operated by Japan Railways (JR). Operating at maximum speeds of up to 320 km/h (200 mph), it is renowned for its world-class punctuality, exceptional safety record, and expansive reach across Honshu, Kyushu, and Hokkaido.

title: The Enoden (Enoshima Electric Railway)
description: The Enoden (Enoshima Electric Railway) is a charming 10-kilometer heritage railway connecting Kamakura and Fujisawa stations in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. Famous for its coastal views, retro cars, and segments where it runs directly through narrow residential streets, the 34-minute ride features 15 stops.
